Sport|Olympics

TheParisParalympicsbegin today, offeringTeam GBmore chances to win medals this summer.

After a stunning Olympic Games in Paris, the action starts in the French capital following what promises to be another dramatic opening ceremony.

Great Britain finished second at the delayed Tokyo Games last time out and have now been set a target of 100-140 medals by UK Sport.

Here’s everything you need to know about where to watch.

TV channel:The live action can be watched free-to-air in the UK on Channel 4, which has promised more than 1,300 hours of coverage.

The broadcaster is currently showing documentaries to pave the way and will be showing the coverage on television and online.

Unlike at the Olympics, where the BBC had only limited broadcasting, Channel 4 will be showing every event with up to 18 streams accessible online.

There will also be a nightly highlights show.

Live stream:Channel 4’s website and app will provide a free live stream service online.

title: How to watch Paralympics 2024 FOR FREE: TV channel and live stream for Paris Games | The Standard
author: Standard Sport
description: All you need to know about where to watch the action over the next fortnight
